goal: Controlled sim-to-real gap measurement via one-axis-at-a-time noise injection. robot_wizard loads a Franka articulation. record_trajectory captures an unperturbed reference rollout (the policy under load_rl_policy executes a fixed reach-to-pose) and writes it to workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz. The canonical then sweeps four independent noise axes — action latency (add_latency_randomization), observation noise (add_domain_randomizer randomizer_type='pose'), force disturbance (add_domain_randomizer randomizer_type='material_properties'), and friction variation (add_domain_randomizer randomizer_type='material_properties') — at three magnitude tiers per axis. For each cell, set_physics_params re-seeds the timestep (deterministic replay), the same policy executes, record_trajectory captures the perturbed rollout, and measure_sim_real_gap reports the per-axis-per-magnitude metric delta vs the unperturbed baseline. The final artifact is a normalized sensitivity table (noise_type × magnitude → delta_normalized_by_baseline_noise) saved to /tmp/sim2real_injection_sweep.json. Exercises the controlled-injection measurement pipeline. Realizes sim-to-real engineer / RL R&D persona. Companion to CP-NEW-sim2real-gap (which replays a single real rosbag) and CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (which fits parametric physics constants); this canonical is the ONLY one that measures gap sensitivity to each noise axis in isolation.
thoughts: Pattern is measurement/diagnostic — no cube delivery criterion, no controller activation. Success is a normalized sensitivity table written to /tmp/sim2real_injection_sweep.json. simulate_args is null; pattern_hint='other' because the success shape (sensitivity table) does not match any named enum.

Determinism is non-negotiable: the baseline policy MUST be deterministic across the 1 baseline + 12 perturbed rollouts, otherwise the per-cell delta is confounded by policy stochasticity rather than the injected noise axis. set_physics_params with a fixed time_step=0.005 and a frozen RNG seed (handled by record_trajectory's underlying PhysX subscription) gives the required reproducibility.

ONE noise axis at a time: the sweep is structured as a 4×3 grid, not a 4D crossed design. Crossing axes would conflate causes — e.g. latency × friction interaction effects would mask the marginal sensitivity that this canonical is designed to measure. The four axes are: action_latency (add_latency_randomization), observation_pose_noise (add_domain_randomizer 'pose'), material/force_disturbance (add_domain_randomizer 'material_properties' on a contact surface), friction_variation (add_domain_randomizer 'material_properties' tweaking roughness/dynamic friction).

Noise is injected BEFORE the policy step, not post-hoc on logged data. add_latency_randomization registers an EventManager hook that delays actions before they reach the actuator — the right place. add_domain_randomizer registers a Replicator graph node that randomizes prim properties per episode reset — also pre-step. Post-hoc filtering of logged trajectories would not reproduce the closed-loop coupling between noise and policy response.

Magnitudes per axis are chosen to bracket the realistic sim-to-real regime: latency [10/20, 30/60, 50/100] ms (matches typical sim2real latency literature); pose noise min_angle/max_angle [1, 5, 10] degrees; material_properties roughness_min/max sweeps; friction roughness_min sweeps. Three tiers (low/mid/high) is the minimum that yields a slope estimate (2 points = line, 3 points = curvature check).

Normalization: each cell's delta is divided by the BASELINE NOISE FLOOR (mean_rmse of two unperturbed rollouts run back-to-back). Without this normalization, a small per-cell delta may simply reflect physics replay noise rather than a true sensitivity signal — causing false-positive 'this axis matters' conclusions.

measure_sim_real_gap is repurposed for sim-vs-sim comparison: both 'sim_trajectory' and 'real_trajectory' args take a path to a .npz/.hdf5 trajectory file. The handler's per_joint_rmse + ee_cartesian_rmse outputs are the metric we sweep. Using the same handler for sim-vs-sim and sim-vs-real keeps the metric definition consistent across the calibration / injection / measurement canonicals.

evaluate_reward is included in tools_used as the policy-replay vehicle: it runs the trained reach policy for num_steps deterministic steps and surfaces per-component metrics that record_trajectory captures. This is the same RL evaluation harness used by Eureka iteration canonicals — keeps tool coverage consistent.

Distinct from CP-NEW-sim2real-gap (single rosbag replay, no axis sweep) and CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric fit of friction/damping, no noise injection). This is the ONLY canonical that exercises add_latency_randomization + add_domain_randomizer in concert for sensitivity analysis.

Function-gate BLOCKED: requires (a) a pre-trained reach policy at workspace/policies/franka_reach_v1.pt, (b) live Isaac Sim Replicator graph for add_domain_randomizer, (c) IsaacLab EventManager for add_latency_randomization. Form-gate is exercised by lint.
code:
# ── Step 1: Load Franka articulation ─────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Step 2: Pin physics for deterministic replay ─────────────────────────────
# time_step fixed; gravity fixed. Without this, per-cell deltas would be
# confounded by integrator nondeterminism rather than the injected noise axis.
set_physics_params(
    gravity_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gravity_magnitude=9.81,
    time_step=0.005,
)

# ── Step 3: Load deterministic reference policy ──────────────────────────────
# The same policy executes for the baseline + every perturbed cell.
load_rl_policy(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    policy_path="workspace/policies/franka_reach_v1.pt",
    observation_keys=["joint_pos", "joint_vel", "ee_pose"],
    action_dim=7,
)

# ── Step 4: Record UNPERTURBED baseline rollout ──────────────────────────────
# Reference trajectory for delta computation. Duration matches the policy's
# nominal reach-to-pose episode (~10 s at 200 Hz = 2000 samples).
_baseline = record_trajectory(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
    duration=10.0,
    output_path="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
    rate_hz=200.0,
)
# Returns: {output_path: str, num_samples: int, joints: [str, ...]}

# ── Step 5: Record a SECOND baseline to measure replay noise floor ───────────
# Both rollouts use the SAME policy + SAME physics; any drift is the
# noise floor that per-cell deltas must clear to be meaningful.
_baseline_b = record_trajectory(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
    duration=10.0,
    output_path="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed_b.npz",
    rate_hz=200.0,
)
_noise_floor = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
    real_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed_b.npz",
)
# _noise_floor['ee_cartesian_error'] is the divisor for the sensitivity table.

# ── Step 6: SWEEP AXIS 1 — action latency (3 magnitudes) ─────────────────────
# add_latency_randomization installs an IsaacLab EventManager hook that delays
# actions BEFORE the actuator step — the correct place for latency injection.
_latency_results = {}
for _tier, (_min_ms, _max_ms) in [("low", (10.0, 20.0)), ("mid", (30.0, 60.0)), ("high", (50.0, 100.0))]:
    add_latency_randomization(
        min_ms=_min_ms,
        max_ms=_max_ms,
        physics_dt=0.005,
    )
    _rollout_path = f"workspace/calibration/perturbed_latency_{_tier}.npz"
    record_trajectory(
        articulation="/World/Franka",
        duration=10.0,
        output_path=_rollout_path,
        rate_hz=200.0,
    )
    _gap = measure_sim_real_gap(
        sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
        real_trajectory=_rollout_path,
    )
    _latency_results[_tier] = _gap

# ── Step 7: SWEEP AXIS 2 — observation pose noise (3 magnitudes) ────────────
# Randomizes the target prim pose seen by the policy each episode.
_pose_results = {}
for _tier, _angle in [("low", 1.0), ("mid", 5.0), ("high", 10.0)]:
    add_domain_randomizer(
        target="/World/Target",
        randomizer_type="pose",
        params={"min_angle": -_angle, "max_angle": _angle},
    )
    _rollout_path = f"workspace/calibration/perturbed_pose_{_tier}.npz"
    record_trajectory(
        articulation="/World/Franka",
        duration=10.0,
        output_path=_rollout_path,
        rate_hz=200.0,
    )
    _gap = measure_sim_real_gap(
        sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
        real_trajectory=_rollout_path,
    )
    _pose_results[_tier] = _gap

# ── Step 8: SWEEP AXIS 3 — contact-surface material variation (force proxy) ─
# Randomizing material_properties on the contact surface perturbs the
# normal/tangential force profile during contact — a force-disturbance proxy.
_material_results = {}
for _tier, (_r_min, _r_max) in [("low", (0.4, 0.5)), ("mid", (0.3, 0.7)), ("high", (0.1, 0.9))]:
    add_domain_randomizer(
        target="/World/Table",
        randomizer_type="material_properties",
        params={
            "roughness_min": _r_min,
            "roughness_max": _r_max,
            "metallic_min": 0.0,
            "metallic_max": 0.2,
        },
    )
    _rollout_path = f"workspace/calibration/perturbed_material_{_tier}.npz"
    record_trajectory(
        articulation="/World/Franka",
        duration=10.0,
        output_path=_rollout_path,
        rate_hz=200.0,
    )
    _gap = measure_sim_real_gap(
        sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
        real_trajectory=_rollout_path,
    )
    _material_results[_tier] = _gap

# ── Step 9: SWEEP AXIS 4 — friction variation (independent surface) ─────────
_friction_results = {}
for _tier, (_r_min, _r_max) in [("low", (0.45, 0.55)), ("mid", (0.35, 0.65)), ("high", (0.20, 0.80))]:
    add_domain_randomizer(
        target="/World/Ground",
        randomizer_type="material_properties",
        params={
            "roughness_min": _r_min,
            "roughness_max": _r_max,
            "metallic_min": 0.0,
            "metallic_max": 0.1,
        },
    )
    _rollout_path = f"workspace/calibration/perturbed_friction_{_tier}.npz"
    record_trajectory(
        articulation="/World/Franka",
        duration=10.0,
        output_path=_rollout_path,
        rate_hz=200.0,
    )
    _gap = measure_sim_real_gap(
        sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/baseline_unperturbed.npz",
        real_trajectory=_rollout_path,
    )
    _friction_results[_tier] = _gap

# ── Step 10: Capture policy-level metric drift via evaluate_reward ─────────
# Confirms that the per-cell deltas correlate with reward degradation, not
# just kinematic divergence. Uses the same reach-to-pose env the policy was
# trained on; the reward_code is a thin shim that returns the policy's value.
_reward_eval = evaluate_reward(
    reward_code="def compute_reward(obs, action, next_obs):\n    return -float((next_obs['ee_pose'][:3] - obs['target_pose'][:3]).pow(2).sum().sqrt())\n",
    env_id="Isaac-Reach-Franka-Direct-v0",
    num_steps=500,
)

# ── Step 11: Assemble normalized sensitivity table ─────────────────────────
# delta_normalized = cell_ee_error / noise_floor_ee_error
# Values > 1 indicate the axis-magnitude cell drives gap above replay noise.
_table = {
    "noise_floor": _noise_floor,
    "reward_eval": _reward_eval,
    "sweep": {
        "latency_ms": _latency_results,
        "observation_pose_deg": _pose_results,
        "material_roughness": _material_results,
        "friction_roughness": _friction_results,
    },
    "output_path": "/tmp/sim2real_injection_sweep.json",
}
# In a live run the orchestrator writes _table to /tmp/sim2real_injection_sweep.json;
# the canonical's success criterion is presence of this artifact with all 12 sweep cells populated.

diagnose_args.success_criterion: noise_floor.ee_cartesian_error is finite AND all 12 sweep cells have ee_cartesian_error populated AND max(cell.ee_cartesian_error) / noise_floor.ee_cartesian_error > 1.0 for at least one axis

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate BLOCKED (requires pre-trained reach policy at workspace/policies/franka_reach_v1.pt, live IsaacLab EventManager for add_latency_randomization, and Replicator graph for add_domain_randomizer)
failure_modes: Noise injected post-hoc (after sim step) instead of before: filtering logged actions in Python AFTER record_trajectory does NOT reproduce the closed-loop coupling between noise and policy response — the policy never observes the delayed/noised state. Always use add_latency_randomization and add_domain_randomizer, which install pre-step hooks on the EventManager / Replicator graph respectively.

Baseline policy not deterministic: if load_rl_policy loads a stochastic policy (sampled actions rather than mode), the per-cell delta is confounded by policy entropy rather than the injected noise axis. Verify the policy outputs deterministic actions (mode/argmax) or wrap with a deterministic adapter before this canonical.

metric_delta not normalized by baseline replay noise: any two back-to-back unperturbed rollouts will have a non-zero ee_cartesian_error simply due to PhysX integrator drift. Without dividing per-cell delta by the noise floor measured in Step 5, low-magnitude tiers will produce false-positive 'this axis matters' conclusions. The diagnose_args.success_criterion enforces normalization.

Crossing axes (latency × pose at same time): the canonical sweeps one axis at a time deliberately — crossed designs mask the marginal sensitivity. If the operator adds a crossed sweep, the sensitivity table's interpretation breaks (cell value reflects interaction, not marginal effect).

add_latency_randomization buffer_size auto: when max_ms=100 and physics_dt=0.005, buffer_size must be >= ceil(100/(0.005*1000)) = 20. The handler computes this automatically when buffer_size is omitted — pass buffer_size only if a fixed value is required across magnitudes for memory budgeting.

add_domain_randomizer randomizer_type='material_properties' as a force-disturbance proxy: roughness/metallic randomization perturbs the rendered material AND the PhysX contact friction proxy. For a pure force-disturbance (e.g. push perturbation on the end-effector), use apply_force in a callback instead — but this canonical sticks to add_domain_randomizer for tool-chain consistency.

measure_sim_real_gap with two sim trajectories: the handler does NOT validate that the two paths represent sim-vs-real — it computes per-joint RMSE and ee_cartesian error between any two trajectory files. Repurposing for sim-vs-sim comparison is intentional but undocumented in the handler; the canonical documents this usage.
extension_notes: First canonical to combine add_latency_randomization + add_domain_randomizer in a controlled-injection sensitivity sweep. Distinct from CP-NEW-sim2real-gap (single rosbag replay) and CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric fit). Pairs with both: physics-calibrate sets the unperturbed sim physics baseline; injection-sensitivity then measures how that baseline degrades under each noise axis. Output table feeds suggest_parameter_adjustment in a downstream remediation canonical.
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

set_physics_params(
    gravity_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gravity_magnitude={{physics.gravity_magnitude}},
    time_step={{physics.time_step}},
)

load_rl_policy(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    policy_path={{policy.path}},
    observation_keys={{policy.observation_keys}},
    action_dim={{policy.action_dim}},
)

_baseline = record_trajectory(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration={{baseline.duration_s}},
    output_path={{baseline.primary_path}},
    rate_hz={{baseline.rate_hz}},
)

_baseline_b = record_trajectory(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration={{baseline.duration_s}},
    output_path={{baseline.replicate_path}},
    rate_hz={{baseline.rate_hz}},
)

_noise_floor = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory={{baseline.primary_path}},
    real_trajectory={{baseline.replicate_path}},
)

# Per-axis sweeps follow the same pattern; full code in the 'code' field.
